title: The overnight build pattern that shipped my app

slides.headline: I shipped an app while sleeping
slides.visual_note: Moon/night sky background, laptop with code on screen

slides.headline: The problem with building apps
slides.body: You sit down. 4 hours pass. You've built 20% of one feature. Repeat for weeks.
slides.visual_note: Clock showing time passing, frustrated developer silhouette

slides.headline: The overnight build pattern
slides.body: Write a detailed spec (PRD). Hand it to Claude Code. Go to sleep. Wake up to a working app.

slides.headline: Step 1: Write the PRD
slides.body: App name. Core feature (ONE). User flow (5 screens max). Tech stack. Monetization. 30 minutes max.
slides.visual_note: PRD document mockup with bullet points

slides.headline: Step 2: Set guardrails
slides.body: Tell the AI: No commits to main without tests. Stop if you hit a blocker twice. Log all decisions.

slides.headline: Step 3: Launch the loop
slides.body: Run: claude --continue. Check progress.md in the morning. Fix blockers. Repeat.

slides.headline: What I shipped overnight
slides.body: PrayerLock: Focus timer for Muslims. WalkToUnlock: Walk to dismiss alarms. StudyLock: Block apps until quiz complete.

slides.headline: The math
slides.body: Traditional: 40 hours over 2 weeks. Overnight pattern: 2 hours setup + 8 hours sleep. Ships 10x faster.

slides.headline: This only works if
slides.body: Your PRD is specific. Your scope is small. You use guardrails. You check the logs before pushing live.

cta_slide.headline: I share every overnight build in real time
